title: Double-cooked Delightful Japanese Cauliflower Katsudon
description: A Shojin-friendly Japanese dish featuring spicy, crisp cauliflower in a savory onion broth. This one-pot meal is a delight for the senses and can be served as a delightful side dish or main course.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the soy sauce, sushi vinegar, and spicy miso paste, stirring to combine.
Add the cauliflower florets and cook for 5 minutes, or until they start to tenderize.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the cauliflower is tender.
Stir in the brown sugar and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer the pot to a broiler or oven and cook at 400°F (200°C) for 5 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Sprinkle the toasted sesame seeds, green onions, and nori pieces on top of the cauliflower.
Serve hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
